Phase-relevant assets

The account can have one overall health read while still showing different adoption phases by function. This section is the operating view AIOMs use to see what each group needs next.

Top 3 workflows

Priority workflows driving account health

Required on every account

These workflows explain whether the account is healthy, blocked, or ready to scale. They sit inside the phase model rather than replacing it.

Each workflow should show whether it already exists, needs to be created, or needs redesign because AI is changing how the work should happen.

Commercial guardrails

These are the AIOM-friendly guardrails. Under the hood, Assistant + Agents health and Prism-derived knowledge now feed the same recommendation system rather than sitting in a separate technical dashboard.

Open Prism

This keeps the AIOM operating surface focused while linking directly to live Prism in Glean. Account-specific Prism view links should only be added after the exact view URLs are verified.

Document library by adoption stage

Templates stay phase-aware, but the recommendation engine picks the one doc you should open first.

Agents, skills, and launchers

AIOM tool library

Launch behavior is explicit, but the tools stay inside the familiar AIOM workflow rather than becoming a separate technical experience.

Client health

Usage, risk, and AIOM watch items

Canvas-safe inline view

This tab combines usage metrics, account-memory signals, and current worries so the AIOM can see what is healthy, what is slipping, and what deserves action.

Usage metrics
What is worrying
Health briefing card
Session capture only
Notes are held in memory while this artifact is open.
Top signals informing Client Health
Signal history
Copy into Prism

Save this summary into Prism after the customer touch so the account memory includes the recommended action, actual outcome, remaining blocker, commitments, and follow-up loop.

Canvas interactive content does not provide persistent browser storage, so capture notes are session-only in this artifact. Durable notes should be copied into the account workspace or captured through a Glean Agent flow.

Design mockup of the AI Fluency layer. This view previews the planned sponsor report (Northstar Enterprise sample data). It is a static placeholder until the live integration ships in Q2/Q3 — not required for V1.
72% Average
fluency
Your organizational AI Fluency

Your Organizational AI Fluency

Northstar Enterprise shows clear cohort-level movement from Time 1 to Time 2. Fluency improved materially, and the readiness and impact measures moved in the same direction.

72% average fluency

This view uses the same results surface as the individual profile. The only admin layer is filtering: choose a function, level, or exposure group to update the cohort movement and profile shape.

AI Fluency lift from baseline to follow-up.
Impact moved +14 pts
What this means for the AIOM
Spread the strongest behaviors and run a leadership reinforcement sprint.
Use the strongest dimension as the proof-of-value example, then target the lowest dimension with a role-specific motion.

Executive summary
Fluency is the capability lever in this cohort.
The active filter is All respondents. Use this view to decide where a team, level, or exposure group needs a different enablement motion.
Transformation strategy
Build fluency to raise readiness, then convert readiness into impact.
The pathway below uses the selected cohort values, avoiding a generic scenario that is detached from the organization view.
Capability lever
AI Fluency
72% in the selected cohort view.
↓
Readiness shift
Attitude + Intent
70% in the selected cohort view.
↓
Business outcome
Perceived Impact
64% in the selected cohort view.
AI Attitude
72%
3.58 out of 5
Behavioral Intent
69%
3.45 out of 5
Perceived Impact
64%
3.20 out of 5
Strategy for this view

Scale what is working in Confidence, then focus enablement on Leadership Reinforcement.

At the organization level, spread the strongest behavior patterns while targeting the weakest fluency dimension with role-specific enablement.

01
Turn confidence into visible examples
Publish two examples from this team that show what strong confidence looks like in an actual workflow.
02
Run a leadership reinforcement practice sprint
Pick one recurring workflow and give managers a 30-minute exercise that targets leadership reinforcement with before-and-after examples.
03
Create a weekly reinforcement loop
Ask leaders to review one AI-assisted work artifact each week and name the behavior they want the team to repeat.
Strategic insights
Immediate focus
Do now

Make leadership sponsorship visible in the work

Embed AI expectations into team reviews, planning, and recognition so people see that usage is institutionally supported, not optional side experimentation.

When leadership signals are weak, even capable teams hesitate to operationalize AI consistently.

Prioritize one or two role-critical AI workflows

Pick the workflows where AI should become the default starting point, then redesign enablement around those moments instead of broad awareness.

Organizations build fluency faster when people repeatedly apply AI in real work instead of treating it as a general capability topic.

Capability build
Build next

Shift from access and awareness to operating discipline

Move beyond general encouragement and codify where AI use is expected, how good usage is reviewed, and which wins should be scaled across teams.

At this maturity level, the next gains come from reinforcement and consistency more than from introductory education.

Operating scale
Scale

Equip managers to reinforce the new way of working

Give frontline leaders simple language, examples, and review habits so teams hear consistent signals about where AI belongs in the workflow.

Leadership reinforcement is what turns isolated adoption into durable team-level behavior change.

Starter schema

Data model

This is the underlying contract that powers the Adoption OS intelligence surface.

This stays secondary so AIOMs can trust the interface without being forced into a model-first workflow.
